rationale: Bug fix with full context from prior investigation. User investigated the issue, confirmed root cause, and approved the exact fix plan.

problem.summary: 14/16 Anthropic skills missing from vskill search. Dedup blocks re-discovery after initial crawl. Cron only runs npm source.

solution.summary: Bypass dedup for vendor-orgs source in processRepo. Add vendor-orgs to hourly cron sources.

architecture.summary: 2 files: github-discovery.ts (conditional in processRepo), build-worker-entry.ts (add vendor-orgs to cron). Existing KV shard update handles indexing.

testing.summary: Unit tests for dedup bypass in existing test file. Post-deploy verification via npx vskill find pptx.

scope.summary: 2 production files + 1 test file. No schema changes, no new endpoints, no UI.

integrations.summary: No new integrations. Uses existing GitHub API discovery, existing WORKER_SELF_REFERENCE submission path, existing KV search index auto-update on publish.

ui-ux.summary: N/A — no UI changes. This is a backend discovery pipeline fix. Search results will naturally include vendor skills once discovery processes them.

performance.summary: Negligible impact. Vendor repos total ~7 orgs, ~50 skills. Skipping dedup for these adds at most 50 re-submissions per hourly cron run. GitHub API calls already happen via the vendor-orgs source function.

security.summary: No security implications. Vendor skills bypass security scanning by design (VENDOR_ORGS are trusted, auto-CERTIFIED). The dedup bypass only affects the discovery step, not the trust/certification pipeline.

edge-cases.summary: If a vendor skill is deleted from the repo, re-submission will fail at fetchRepoFiles (404) and the existing skill record remains — acceptable behavior. If GitHub API rate limits hit, the vendor-orgs source already has backoff/retry logic.
